body: {"data":[{"_id":"BE-1020835-A5","data":{"title":"METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING A PLANT EXTRACT, I.H.B. FROM DESMODIUM.","nbCompounds":0}},{"_id":"CA-2868128-A1","data":{"title":"Method for producing a plant extract from desmodium and its extract","nbCompounds":40,"abstract":"This invention relates to a method for producing a plant extract, quantified on pinitol, wherein a plant Desmodium adscendens is selected from the Desmodium family, wherein a fraction is extracted from Desmodium plant parts, wherein a plant extract is derived from said fraction thereof, which is remarkable in that a characterised extract is derived from Desmodium adscendens, from which a preparation of said plant extract is quantified on pinitol."}},{"_id":"CN-101395259-A","data":{"title":"Rifamycin analogs and uses thereof","nbCompounds":173,"abstract":"The present invention features rifamycin analogs and methods of using these compounds to treat a variety of microbial infections."}},{"_id":"CN-101404980-A","data":{"title":"Preparation Technology of Macromolecular Microspheres","nbCompounds":2538,"abstract":"Microspheres are produced by contacting an aqueous solution of a protein or other macromolecule with an organic solvent and a counterion and freezing the solution. The microspheres are useful for preparing drugs of a specified size."}},{"_id":"CN-101501036-A","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, processes for their preparation and their use as medicaments","nbCompounds":745,"abstract":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidine derivatives represented by formula (I) are potent mGluR5 regulators and are useful for the prevention of acute and chronic neurological disorders, wherein Y  1  , Y  2  , Y  3  , and Y  4  represent N or C-, at least two of the groups Y  1  to Y  4  represent carbon atoms, R  1  represents chlorine or bromine, R  2  to R  7  represent for example hydrogen, methyl or ethyl; and R  10  and R  11  are independently represents for example hydrogen or C  1  -C  6  alkyl."}},{"_id":"CN-101601642-A","data":{"title":"A kind of vesicle class drug-supplying system and application thereof","nbCompounds":197,"abstract":"The invention belongs to field of pharmaceutical preparations, a kind of vesicle class drug-supplying system is disclosed, it adopts the preparation of following method, and described method comprises that successively (1) is the underlying membrane material with the surfactant, is that the aquation medium prepares blank vesicle with the polyanion compound solution of nitrogen atom; (2) blank vesicle is set up transmembrane gradient and is got final product, wherein, the polyanion compound of described nitrogen atom is to contain nitrogen-atoms and following anionic chemical compound more than two in the molecule structure: carboxyl, sulfate, sulfonic group, phosphate, citron acidic group or phosphonate group.The drug-supplying system that the inventive method is set up has that the medicine carrying scope is wide, entrapment efficiency is high and the characteristics of good stability, and can reduce drug toxicity."}},{"_id":"CN-101977612-A","data":{"title":"Application of phenol glycoside derivatives in the preparation of anti-cell proliferative disease composition","nbCompounds":51,"abstract":"The invention discloses the use of a class of phenolic glycoside derivatives for preparing compositions for preventing or treating abnormal cell proliferation diseases. The phenol glycoside derivatives can be used as anti-abnormal cell proliferation drugs alone or in combination, and can be combined with other anti-abnormal cell proliferation drugs to enhance drug efficacy."}},{"_id":"CN-101991536-A","data":{"title":"A kind of vesicle with inner and outer aqueous phase gradient difference and its preparation method and application","nbCompounds":193,"abstract":"The invention belongs to the field of pharmaceutical preparations and discloses vesicles with inner and outer aqueous-phase gradient difference. The vesicles are prepared by treating blank vesicles by adopting an ion exchange method or combining the ion exchange method and a dialysis method or an ultrafiltration method. The invention also provides a preparation method and application of the vesicles; and the preparation method comprises the following steps of: preparing the blank vesicles; treating the prepared blank vesicles by using an ion exchanger; and performing elution treatment so as to prepare the vesicles with inner and outer aqueous-phase gradient difference. The prepared vesicles can be mixed with medicinal solution to realize active medicament carrying so as to prepare the pharmaceutical preparations. The prepared preparations also can be subjected to ion exchange treatment or freeze-drying treatment. The provided vesicles have higher inner and outer aqueous-phase gradient difference, can realize ion gradient medicament carrying of the vesicles and achieve higher encapsulation rate; meanwhile, the preparation method is simple and is low in cost, and the vesicles can be better applied to preparing liposome gel, magnetic liposome and nanoparticles/nanogel."}},{"_id":"CN-102107006-A","data":{"title":"Drug conjugates for treating diabetes and their medical use","nbCompounds":238,"abstract":"The present invention discloses a natural conjugated drug for treating diabetes and other blood vessel diseases and its medical application. body coupled. It can be used to prepare finished medicine for treating diabetes, and has the functions of lowering blood sugar, blood pressure, blood fat, anti-platelet aggregation and anti-atherosclerosis. After entering the human body, the conjugated drug can be enzymatically hydrolyzed in the body to release nitric oxide and natural drug ingredients, which not only exerts the advantages of natural drugs with less toxic and side effects and long-lasting effects, but also has the characteristics of fast and strong nitric oxide activity . It has a wide range of uses in medicine."}},{"_id":"CN-102146057-A","data":{"title":"C19-diterpenoid alkaloid, preparation method of C19-diterpenoid alkaloid, medical composite with C19-diterpenoid alkaloid used as active ingredient and application of medical composite","nbCompounds":38,"abstract":"The present invention relates to C  19  -diterpene alkaloids of formula I and their pharmaceutically acceptable salts having cardiotonic and anti-heart failure activity, processes for their preparation, medicaments containing them and their use as cardiotonic and anti-heart failure Drug use.  In formula I, R  1  is hydrogen, hydroxyl or methoxy; R  2  is hydrogen or methyl; R  3  , R  4  and R  5  are hydrogen or hydroxyl."}},{"_id":"CN-102146057-B","data":{"title":"C19-diterpenoid alkaloid, preparation method of C19-diterpenoid alkaloid, medical composite with C19-diterpenoid alkaloid used as active ingredient and application of medical composite","nbCompounds":26,"abstract":"The invention relates to C19-diterpenoid alkaloid which has cardiotonic and hart failure-resistance activities and is shown in the formula I, pharmaceutically acceptable salts of the C19-diterpenoid alkaloid, preparation methods of the C19-diterpenoid alkaloid and the salts, and medicines containing the C19-diterpenoid alkaloid and the salts, as well as application of the C19-diterpenoid alkaloid and the salts as medicines with cardiotonic and hart failure-resistance effects. In the formula I, R1 is hydrogen, hydroxyl or methoxyl; R2 is hydrogen or methyl; and R3, R4 and R5 represent hydrogen or hydroxyl."}},{"_id":"CN-102276529-A","data":{"title":"Preparation method of salsoline","nbCompounds":23,"abstract":"The invention provides a preparation method of salsoline. The preparation method comprises the following process steps of: taking fine powder of a salsola raw material, adding to an alkaline alcohol to infiltrate for 4-8 hours, adding to an extraction vessel, and extracting through supercritical CO2 extraction; dissolving the extract with 2-3% hydrochloric acid aqueous solution, filtering, extracting filtrate with chloroform, taking a chloroform layer, recovering chloroform, and drying to obtain a crude product; and separating salsoline in the crude product by using a high-speed countercurrent chromatograph and taking an ammonia aqueous solution (pH=8) of n-hexane-chloroform-methanol as a solvent system, collecting salsola components, concentrating and drying to obtain high-purity salsoline. The preparation method provided by the invention is simple to operate, is low in pollution, and is easy to realize industrial amplification; and the salsoline prepared by the preparation method provided by the invention has high purity."}},{"_id":"CN-102432661-A","data":{"title":"A kind of preparation method of high-purity closed-linked solanidine diendiol","nbCompounds":6,"abstract":"The invention belongs to the field of medical technology and relates to a method for preparing high-purity closed solanine diol. The method comprises crushing the whole herb of Veratrum angustifolium, extracting with an organic solvent, enriching with a macroporous adsorption resin column, separating and purifying with a high-speed countercurrent chromatography, and drying to obtain a closed solanine diol monomer finished product. The method is simple to operate, easy to control, has little environmental pollution, and the obtained product has high purity and is easy to mass produce."}},{"_id":"CN-102659674-A","data":{"title":"Process for extracting salsoline from Common Russianthistle Herb","nbCompounds":3}},{"_id":"CN-102659779-A","data":{"title":"Method extracting salsoline A from salsola collina","nbCompounds":6}},{"_id":"CN-102813929-A","data":{"title":"Low concentration PEG lipid derivative and its application","nbCompounds":165,"abstract":"The invention belongs to the technical field of medicine, relates to a low-concentration PEG-lipid derivative and an application thereof, particularly to a prescription composition and an application thereof which can relieving or avoiding accelerated blood clearance (ABC) of PEGylation preparations. According to the low-concentration PEG-lipid derivative and the application thereof, the low-content PEG-lipid derivative is used for modifying liquid particle preparations such as emulsions, liposome, vesicles, micro emulsions, micelles and nano-particles, PEG and lipid chain segments are connected through ester bonds, amido bonds or ether bonds in the PEG-lipid derivative, and the molecular weight of the PEG is 200-20000 Dalton. Preparations prepared by the PEG-lipid derivative can only cause slight or cannot cause the ABC, namely, can reduce or avoid the ABC after repeated injection. The low-concentration PEG does not reduce in vitro anti-tumor activity of preparations, and stability of the preparation can be improved if the low-concentration PEG-lipid derivative is added, particularly shaking instability of antagonistic emulsions."}},{"_id":"CN-102813929-B","data":{"title":"Low concentration PEG lipid derivative and its application","nbCompounds":163,"abstract":"The invention belongs to the technical field of medicine, relates to a low-concentration PEG-lipid derivative and an application thereof, particularly to a prescription composition and an application thereof which can relieving or avoiding accelerated blood clearance (ABC) of PEGylation preparations. According to the low-concentration PEG-lipid derivative and the application thereof, the low-content PEG-lipid derivative is used for modifying liquid particle preparations such as emulsions, liposome, vesicles, micro emulsions, micelles and nano-particles, PEG and lipid chain segments are connected through ester bonds, amido bonds or ether bonds in the PEG-lipid derivative, and the molecular weight of the PEG is 200-20000 Dalton. Preparations prepared by the PEG-lipid derivative can only cause slight or cannot cause the ABC, namely, can reduce or avoid the ABC after repeated injection. The low-concentration PEG does not reduce in vitro anti-tumor activity of preparations, and stability of the preparation can be improved if the low-concentration PEG-lipid derivative is added, particularly shaking instability of antagonistic emulsions."}},{"_id":"CN-102846474-A","data":{"title":"A device and method for preparing vesicles with gradient difference between inner and outer water phases","nbCompounds":179,"abstract":"The invention belongs to the field of a pharmaceutical preparation and discloses a method and a device for rapidly preparing a vesica (including liposome) with inner and outer water phase gradient difference in a large scale, and an application thereof. According to the device and the method disclosed by the invention, a membrane separation mechanism, and an ion exchanging and adsorption mechanism are ingeniously combined; and when the property, the ion gradient type and the preparation scale of the vesica with the needed gradient are different, a membrane separation device and an ion exchanging device, which have the different performances, are combined. With the adoption of a membrane separation method, cations, anions, zwitter ions and/or charge macromolecular substances in a vesica mixed suspension hydrated medium are distributed into a dialysis medium; and an ion exchanging method is used for selectively exchanging or cleaning the cations, the anions, the zwitter ions and/or the charge macromolecular substances in the dialysis medium, so that the vesica can establish the greater inner and outer water phase gradient difference in shorter time. The method and the device can be applied to preparing liposome gel, magnetic liposome, and nano grain/nano gel."}},{"_id":"CN-102846474-B","data":{"title":"A device and method for preparing vesicles with gradient difference between inner and outer water phases","nbCompounds":172,"abstract":"The invention belongs to the field of a pharmaceutical preparation and discloses a method and a device for rapidly preparing a vesica (including liposome) with inner and outer water phase gradient difference in a large scale, and an application thereof. According to the device and the method disclosed by the invention, a membrane separation mechanism, and an ion exchanging and adsorption mechanism are ingeniously combined; and when the property, the ion gradient type and the preparation scale of the vesica with the needed gradient are different, a membrane separation device and an ion exchanging device, which have the different performances, are combined. With the adoption of a membrane separation method, cations, anions, zwitter ions and/or charge macromolecular substances in a vesica mixed suspension hydrated medium are distributed into a dialysis medium; and an ion exchanging method is used for selectively exchanging or cleaning the cations, the anions, the zwitter ions and/or the charge macromolecular substances in the dialysis medium, so that the vesica can establish the greater inner and outer water phase gradient difference in shorter time. The method and the device can be applied to preparing liposome gel, magnetic liposome, and nano grain/nano gel."}},{"_id":"CN-102977020-A","data":{"title":"Aconitine-type alkaloid, preparation method thereof, pharmaceutical composition and application using the compound as cardiotonic and anti-heart failure active ingredients","nbCompounds":31,"abstract":"The invention relates to aconitine alkaloids with cardiotonic and anti-heart-failure functions disclosed as Formulae I-IX, pharmaceutically acceptable salts thereof and preparation method of aconitine alkaloids, and a pharmaceutical composition containing the compounds, and application of aconitine alkaloids in cardiotonic and anti-heart-failure medicines."}},{"_id":"CN-103110097-A","data":{"title":"Antihypertensive Diet","nbCompounds":8,"abstract":"The antihypertensive food therapy product is to prevent and treat hyperemia, according to the principles of pathology, pharmacology and combined medication, to discover and innovate the food therapy of the motherland's medicine. Proper combination of fat-free or low-fat melons, vegetables, fruits, meat, eggs and Chinese herbal medicines that can treat both symptoms and root causes are used. It is a kind of antihypertensive food therapy product invented and created by integrating health preservation and antihypertensive, nutritional antihypertensive and joint antihypertensive. It enables various antihypertensive substances to produce synergistic and comprehensive antihypertensive affinity. Develop the interaction between the original drugs in traditional Chinese medicine, and overcome the disadvantages of avoiding fear and killing each other and relying on drugs to lower blood pressure in conventional treatment."}},{"_id":"CN-103156871-A","data":{"title":"A method to avoid the phenomenon of accelerated blood clearance by continuous multiple injections of epirubicin hydrochloride PEGylated liposomes","nbCompounds":56,"abstract":"The invention belongs to the technical field of medicine and relates to a method for avoiding the accelerated blood clearance phenomenon of continuous multiple injections of PEGylated liposomes, in particular to the effect of the first injection of blank liposomes and PEGylated epirubicin hydrochloride liposomes on the secondary injection of PEG The influence of pharmacokinetic behavior of epirubicin hydrochloride in PEGylated liposomes, and the accelerated blood clearance phenomenon of continuous multiple injections of PEGylated liposomes. On this basis, the dosage for reducing or avoiding accelerating blood clearance of PEGylated epirubicin hydrochloride liposomes is provided. The present invention achieves the purpose of avoiding drug release from liposomes and at the same time alleviating or eliminating accelerated blood clearance by changing the dose of epirubicin hydrochloride during the first or continuous injection."}},{"_id":"CN-103169981-A","data":{"title":"A method for eliminating or weakening the phenomenon of accelerated blood clearance","nbCompounds":76,"abstract":"The invention belongs to medicine technical field, and discloses a method for eliminating and reducing generation of accelerated blood clearance phenomenon, which is characterized in that an injection interval of the PEGylation can be selected in a reasonable mode to eliminate or avoid the generation of accelerated blood clearance phenomenon. The injection interval of the PEGylation preparation is no less than 30 days, and preferably no less than 90 days, the PEGylation preparation is a clinical prevention, diagnosis or treatment preparation. The injection administration of the PEGylation preparation can reduce or completely eliminate the accelerated blood clearance phenomenon, thereby the generation of severe organ toxic response or analogous anaphylactic reaction can be avoided, and the pharmaceutical effect of the PEGylation preparation can be ensured."}},{"_id":"CN-103784408-A","data":{"title":"Techniques used to prepare microparticles","nbCompounds":2528,"abstract":"Microspheres are produced by contacting a solution of macromolecules or small molecules in a solvent with an antisolvent and a counterion and cooling the solution. Microspheres can be used to prepare pharmaceuticals, nutraceuticals, cosmetics and the like in specified sizes."}},{"_id":"CN-104507533-B","data":{"title":"Implant device and system for ablation of vessel walls from inside","nbCompounds":5,"abstract":"The present invention relates to systems, devices and methods for ablation of the wall of one or more Pulmonary Veins (PV) from the inside, preferably transmural ablation and preferably at the level of the sinuses. Thereby, one or more implant devices may be implanted in the blood vessel and may subsequently be heated by the external energy providing means."}},{"_id":"CN-104758253-A","data":{"title":"Preparation Technology of Macromolecular Microspheres","nbCompounds":3096,"abstract":"The invention relates to the preparation technology of macromolecular microspheres. Microspheres are produced by contacting an aqueous solution of a protein or other macromolecule with an organic solvent and a counterion and freezing the solution. The microspheres are useful for preparing drugs of a given size."}},{"_id":"CN-105078992-B","data":{"title":"Use of isoquinoline alkaloid derivatives for the preparation of drugs promoting AMPK activity","nbCompounds":16,"abstract":"The invention discloses application of an isoquinoline alkaloid derivative in preparing a medicament with the efficacy of activating phosphorylation adenylate dependent protein kinase (AMPK), wherein the isoquinoline alkaloid derivative can be norsalsoline (salsolinol) or reticuline (reticuline). Wherein the drug activates AMPK, and thus can be used for regulating cell growth and metabolism, and treating AMPK-related diseases."}},{"_id":"CN-105233033-A","data":{"title":"Preparation and preparation method for treating nervous tinnitus","nbCompounds":59,"abstract":"The invention discloses a preparation for treating nervous tinnitus, which is prepared from the following raw materials, all in parts by weight: Cistanche 9-14; 12; Mother of Pearl 5-10; Chicory 7-14; Lip Herb 9-15; Pseudostellaria 8-14; Xihuangcao 6-15; Fishing Stalk 8-13; Peanut Grass 7-15; Corn Silk 10-16; Lemongrass 7-12; Yam 9-13. The excellent effect of the present invention is also manifested in: the present invention organically combines traditional medicinal properties with modern pharmacology of traditional Chinese medicine, utilizes the synergistic effect of various medicinal materials, has the effects of dredging Shaoyang menstruation, clearing ear orifices, relieving tinnitus, nourishing kidney and essence, thereby strengthening the The nutrient supply of the ear nerve can achieve the purpose of eliminating the symptoms of tinnitus."}},{"_id":"CN-107043371-A","data":{"title":"β2-AR agonistic and anti-inflammatory bifunctional alkaloid and its application","nbCompounds":20,"abstract":"The invention discloses the alkaloid shown in a kind of Formulas I or Formula II, its analog, isomers, raceme or derivative； Above-claimed cpd system the present invention it is isolated from purslane first, and study discovery above-claimed cpd there is β 2 AR excitements and the difunctional activity of anti-inflammatory, the diseases associated with inflammation for the respiratory diseases such as treatment asthma and correlation provide useful compound."}},{"_id":"CN-107043371-B","data":{"title":"β2-AR agonistic and anti-inflammatory bifunctional alkaloids and their applications","nbCompounds":24,"abstract":"The invention discloses an alkaloid represented by formula I or formula II, its analog, isomer, racemate or derivative;   The above-mentioned compounds are obtained from Portulaca oleracea for the first time in the present invention, and research finds that the above-mentioned compounds have β2  -  AR agonistic and anti-inflammatory bifunctional activities, and provide useful compounds for the treatment of respiratory diseases such as asthma and related inflammatory diseases. ."}},{"_id":"CN-107427508-A","data":{"title":"Novel use of isoquinoline derivatives for diabetic wound healing","nbCompounds":10,"abstract":"The present invention relates to a method for diabetic wound healing, comprising administering to a subject in need thereof a pharmaceutical composition comprising a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier and a therapeutically effective amount of a compound of formula I, preferably norsalsolinol."}},{"_id":"CN-107850581-A","data":{"title":"Analytical pretreatment methods for samples containing multiple components with significantly different concentrations","nbCompounds":97,"abstract":"An analysis pretreatment method for obtaining an analysis sample in which the concentration of various components contained in the sample is adjusted to a specified range, including: a first holding process, combining a specified amount of the sample with The first solvent is supplied to the solid phase, so that the low-concentration components are held by the solid phase; the first cleaning process, the first solvent is supplied to the solid phase, and the high-concentration components are removed from the solid phase; the second holding process will be less than the first holding process When the above-mentioned sample amount and the second solvent are supplied to the solid phase, the high-concentration components are retained by the solid phase; in the elution process, the components retained by the solid phase are simultaneously extracted with an extraction solvent; the first solvent can dissolve low-concentration components. The component and the high-concentration component have the property of keeping the low-concentration component in the solid phase and the high-concentration component not being held in the solid phase, and the second solvent has the property of keeping the low-concentration component and the high-concentration component in the solid phase."}},{"_id":"CN-107955295-B","data":{"title":"A kind of auxiliary heat stabilizer and its preparation method and application","nbCompounds":11,"abstract":"The invention provides an auxiliary heat stabilizer, a preparation method and application thereof. The auxiliary heat stabilizer is prepared from polyol, dicarboxylic acid and 1,3,5-tris(2-hydroxyethyl)cyanuric acid. The molar ratio of the polyol, the dicarboxylic acid and the 1,3,5-tris(2-hydroxyethyl) cyanuric acid is (1-5):(0.5-2.5):1. Compared with the prior art, the auxiliary heat stabilizer provided by the present invention has a triazine ring and a polyhydroxy structure, and can be applied to a composite calcium-zinc heat stabilizer to improve long-term thermal stability, thereby effectively improving the long-term thermal stability of polyvinyl chloride. and can improve the lubricating properties of polyvinyl chloride melt and reduce torque. The experimental results show that the change time of PVC static aging test using the composite calcium zinc heat stabilizer provided by the invention is not less than 60min, the discoloration time of Congo red test is not less than 51min, and the equilibrium torque is not more than 15.6N·m."}},{"_id":"CN-108391756-A","data":{"title":"Preparation and application of a protein source additive for lactating sows","nbCompounds":11,"abstract":"The present invention provides a protein source additive for lactating sows. The raw materials for the preparation of the protein source additive at least include: amino acids and derivatives thereof, sugars, hydroxyl-containing aromatic rings, tetrahydropyrrole acetic acid, alkaloids, 1-(4 -methoxyphenyl)piperazine, phytosterol and 4-hydroxy-β-damascone. The feed additive provided by the invention can promote animal feed intake, increase feed utilization rate, improve meat quality, and at the same time, without adding antibiotics, can significantly improve animal immunity and antioxidant capacity, reduce morbidity, and is drug-free Residue, the meat is safer and higher quality."}},{"_id":"CN-108872443-A","data":{"title":"A kind of quality detection method of Ginger-roasted Magnolia officinalis","nbCompounds":51,"abstract":"The invention discloses a quality detection method of Ginger Ginger Bark. The method comprises preparation of a sample solution, detection and identification of components in Ginger Ginger Ginger Bark by using UPLC/Q-TOF-MS/MS. The present invention screens out the best mobile phase composition, gradient elution program, flow rate, detection wavelength, chromatographic column and column temperature through a large number of experiments, and screens out the best mass spectrometry conditions through a large number of experiments. Experimental verification shows that the present invention provides The detection method can detect at least 36 kinds of active ingredients such as magnolol and honokiol, so it can comprehensively, objectively and accurately detect and evaluate the quality of ginger-branched magnolia bark, which is of great significance for ensuring clinical efficacy."}},{"_id":"CN-111902146-B","data":{"title":"Medicines for cough","nbCompounds":590,"abstract":"The present invention relates to a medicine for preventing or treating cough, comprising a compound having a P2X4 receptor antagonistic effect, a tautomer, a stereoisomer of the compound, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof or a hydrate or solvate thereof as an active ingredient."}},{"_id":"CN-112142784-A","data":{"title":"Asymmetric reduction of nitrogen-phosphono-protected imines","nbCompounds":30,"abstract":"The invention discloses an asymmetric reduction method of nitrogen-phosphono-protected imine. The nitrogen-phosphono-protected imine is reduced to a chiral amine in a hydrogen atmosphere under the action of a metal catalyst and a base, and the metal catalyst It is prepared from metal iridium complexes and nitrogen-phosphorus chiral ligands. The method provided by the invention has the characteristics of high enantioselectivity, high yield and high transformation number (TON). The method can synthesize various substituted chiral amines, which can be used as important intermediates for the preparation of various drugs, and is of great significance for the production of pharmaceutical industry."}},{"_id":"CN-112142784-B","data":{"title":"Asymmetric reduction of nitrogen-phosphono-protected imines","nbCompounds":31,"abstract":"The invention discloses an asymmetric reduction method of N-phosphono protected imine, wherein the N-phosphono protected imine is reduced into chiral amine in hydrogen atmosphere under the action of a metal catalyst and alkali, and the metal catalyst is prepared from a metal iridium complex and a nitrogen-phosphorus chiral ligand. The method provided by the invention has the characteristics of high enantioselectivity, high yield and high conversion number (TON). The method can synthesize various substituted chiral amines, can be used as an important intermediate for preparing various medicaments, and has important significance for the production of the pharmaceutical industry."}},{"_id":"CN-112159699-A","data":{"title":"A kind of high-efficiency water-soluble magnetic tile release agent and preparation method thereof","nbCompounds":16}},{"_id":"CN-113164791-B","data":{"title":"Topical antimicrobial microemulsion containing fluorescent substances","nbCompounds":87,"abstract":"The present invention discloses a topical antimicrobial composition, comprising: a lipophilic component containing a surfactant system having an HLB value of less than 10; an amphiphilic component containing an antimicrobial compound; an aqueous hydrophilic component; and a fluorescent substance. The composition comprises less than 10% by weight of a lower monohydric alcohol based on the total weight of the composition. The composition is in the form of a microemulsion at room temperature and has a dispersed phase with droplets having a particle size of 5 nm to 400 nm."}},{"_id":"CN-113226378-A","data":{"title":"Antibody-drug conjugates","nbCompounds":319,"abstract":"The present invention relates to antibody-drug conjugates comprising: (i) an antibody or antigen-binding fragment thereof, (ii) a polymer comprising specific repeat units, optionally covalently bound by a linker to one or more biologically active moieties, such as a small molecule drug, and (iii) a polymer-antibody linker moiety, covalently bound to both the polymer and the antibody or antigen-binding fragment thereof. Furthermore, the present invention relates to pharmaceutical compositions comprising the antibody-drug conjugates and to the use of the antibody-drug conjugates in medicine."}},{"_id":"CN-113331233-A","data":{"title":"Buck Sauce","nbCompounds":7,"abstract":"The present invention is the only outstanding contemporary inventor couple Li Wenhua and Zhang Yanqing in China who practice the homology of medicine and food and research and develop various diseases that \"eat diseases\" of human beings, especially hypertension, diabetes, and obesity. Chinese food therapy products, casting Chinese food therapy brands. Discover and innovate traditional Korean hot sauce with antihypertensive effect, adopt modern technology, extract Chinese herbal medicines with double antihypertensive properties to create combined antihypertensive alcohol, properly dissolve in sauce and antihypertensive food, and innovate various raw materials and original medicines There is mutual growth, mutual beard, the same affinity to reduce blood pressure, sweet and delicious, improve the edible value and dietary value of the sauce, and invent and create a kind of sauce to eat more, which can eat high blood pressure, low blood pressure, and eat diseases. Eat healthy, the best antihypertensive sauce. The sauce was the first to enter the dietary therapy routine, which opened up a precedent for Chinese dietary therapy and created a new way for human dietary therapy to lower blood pressure."}},{"_id":"CN-113441185-A","data":{"title":"A kind of ethylene oligomerization catalyst and method for continuous production of 1-hexene and 1-octene","nbCompounds":48,"abstract":"The invention discloses a method for continuous production of 1-hexene and 1-octene, comprising: (1) after mixing a solvent and an alkyl aluminum compound in a mixing tank, they are continuously introduced into a reaction kettle, and ethylene is introduced into the reaction kettle. Under the effect of the catalyzer that feeds, ethylene oligomerization reaction occurs, obtains the reaction solution containing 1-hexene and 1-octene; (2) the reaction solution containing 1-hexene and 1-octene enters through overflow line to the overflow tank reactor; (3) the reaction solution obtained by the overflow tank reactor enters the quenching reactor, and uses alcohols as a quenching agent to quench the reaction to obtain 1-hexene and 1-octene. Wherein, the catalyst used is a solution formed by a metal chromium compound, a PNSiNP ligand and an organic boron compound. In the present invention, under the condition of not changing the reaction temperature, the overflow tank adopts a higher temperature, and the polymer from the reaction kettle can be dissolved in the reaction liquid, thereby reducing the possibility of pipeline blockage and improving the continuous operation time of the device."}},{"_id":"CN-113441185-B","data":{"title":"A kind of ethylene oligomerization catalyst and method for continuous production of 1-hexene and 1-octene","nbCompounds":58,"abstract":"The invention discloses a method for continuous production of 1-hexene and 1-octene, comprising: (1) after mixing a solvent and an alkyl aluminum compound in a mixing tank, they are continuously introduced into a reaction kettle, and ethylene is introduced into the reaction kettle. Under the effect of the catalyzer that feeds, ethylene oligomerization reaction occurs, obtains the reaction solution containing 1-hexene and 1-octene; (2) the reaction solution containing 1-hexene and 1-octene enters through overflow line to the overflow tank reactor; (3) the reaction solution obtained by the overflow tank reactor enters the quenching reactor, and uses alcohols as a quenching agent to quench the reaction to obtain 1-hexene and 1-octene. Wherein, the catalyst used is a solution formed by a metal chromium compound, a PNSiNP ligand and an organic boron compound. In the present invention, under the condition of not changing the reaction temperature, the overflow tank adopts a higher temperature, and the polymer from the reaction kettle can be dissolved in the reaction liquid, thereby reducing the possibility of pipeline blockage and improving the continuous operation time of the device."}},{"_id":"CN-114430738-A","data":{"title":"Programmable polymeric pharmaceuticals","nbCompounds":353,"abstract":"Compounds useful as biologically active compounds are disclosed. The compound has the following structure (I):   or a stereoisomer, tautomer or salt thereof, wherein R  1  , R  2  , R  3  , R  4  , R  5  , L, L  1  , L  2  , M and n are as defined herein. Methods for their preparation and uses of such compounds are also provided."}},{"_id":"CN-115561329-A","data":{"title":"A method for determining the concentration of schisandra ester A and schisandrin B in Fuzheng Huayu preparations","nbCompounds":7,"abstract":"The present invention provides a method for measuring the concentration of schisandrin A and/or schisandra B in tissues, the method comprises the following steps: (1) pretreatment of the tissue sample to be tested, and obtaining the test solution; (2) Schisandrin A and/or Schizandrin standard curve preparation; and (3) liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry detection of Schisandrin A and/or Schizandrin concentration in the liquid to be tested, wherein the liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry The chromatographic conditions used are: chromatographic column: ZORBAX Eclipse Plus C  18  (RRHD) SB-C  18  column (2.1mm×100mm, 1.8μm), mobile phase: aqueous phase (A) is pure water, organic phase (B) is Methanol or acetonitrile; Elution method: elution with the following gradient program: 0-10min, 50%-20%A; 10-19min, 20%-5%A; 19.01min, 95%A; 19.01-22min, 95% A; Among them, the mass spectrometry conditions of the liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry are: electrospray ionization source (ESI), positive ion mode detection, mass scanning range m/z200-500. This method realizes the quantitative detection of active ingredients."}},{"_id":"CN-115561332-A","data":{"title":"Method for measuring concentrations of schisandrin B and deoxyschizandrin in preparation for strengthening body resistance and removing blood stasis in tissues","nbCompounds":8,"abstract":"The present invention provides a method for measuring the concentration of schisandrin B and/or schisandrin A in tissues, the method comprising the following steps: (1) preprocessing the tissue sample to be tested, and obtaining the test solution; (2) Schisandrin B and/or Schizandrin standard curve preparation; and (3) liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry detection of Schisandrin B and/or Schisandrin A concentration in the liquid to be tested, wherein the liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry The chromatographic conditions used are: chromatographic column: Acquity   BEH C  18  column (2.1mm×100mm, 1.7μm), mobile phase: aqueous phase (A) is acid aqueous solution, organic phase (B) is methanol or acetonitrile; elution method: use the following gradient program elution: 0-15min , 60%-5%A; 15-19min, 5%A; 19.01min, 95%A; 19.01-22min, 95%A; wherein, the mass spectrometry condition of the liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry is: electrospray ionization source (ESI), using positive ion mode detection, mass scan range m/z 200‑500. This method realizes the quantitative detection of active ingredients."}},{"_id":"CN-116096370-A","data":{"title":"HPK1 inhibitors and uses thereof","nbCompounds":144,"abstract":"The present invention provides compounds represented by structural formula (I) or formula (II), or pharmaceutically acceptable salts or stereoisomers thereof, which are useful for treating diseases (such as cancer) that can be treated by inhibiting HPK1 activity."}},{"_id":"CN-116437928-A","data":{"title":"Pharmaceutical composition for preventing, suppressing or treating symptoms accompanying allergic reactions","nbCompounds":177,"abstract":"The present invention relates to a pharmaceutical composition for preventing, inhibiting or treating symptoms associated with allergic reactions, which comprises a compound having a P2X4 receptor antagonistic action or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof as an active ingredient."}},{"_id":"CN-117143658-A","data":{"title":"A bio-based environmentally friendly long-lasting microemulsion metal cutting fluid","nbCompounds":5,"abstract":"The application provides a bio-based environment-friendly long-acting microemulsion metal cutting fluid, which comprises base oil and additives, wherein the base fluid comprises the following components in percentage by mass: 40.0 to 50.0 percent of hydrogenated rapeseed oil and 10.0 to 15.0 percent of water; the additive comprises the following components in percentage by mass: the environment-friendly microemulsion metal cutting fluid has excellent lubricity, abrasion resistance, rust resistance, cleaning performance and cooling performance, replaces imported products, and meets the requirements of the high-end machining field on the cutting fluid."}},{"_id":"CN-117562902-A","data":{"title":"A traditional Chinese medicine monomer that inhibits endothelial apoptosis and improves atherosclerosis and its application","nbCompounds":4,"abstract":"The present invention relates to a traditional Chinese medicine monomer that inhibits endothelial apoptosis and improves atherosclerosis and its application. The traditional Chinese medicine monomer is norsoline; its advantages are shown in: norrisine in the present invention As the main component of the water-soluble alkaloids of Chinese monkshood, it has been proven through various experiments that norlinine can delay the pathological process of atherosclerosis induced by Western diet in Apoe  -/-  mice and protect H  2  O  2-  induced in vitro Human umbilical vein endothelial cell apoptosis means that it can improve atherosclerosis by inhibiting the apoptosis of vascular endothelial cells, and effectively prevent and treat diseases caused by endothelial cell apoptosis."}},{"_id":"CN-118480173-A","data":{"title":"Cationic polyester and preparation method and application thereof","nbCompounds":222,"abstract":"The invention discloses a cationic polyester, comprising a copolymer of monomer C, monomer S and monomer M, wherein the copolymer terminal group is modified with a group E; the monomer C comprises cholesterol; the monomer S comprises an organic acid having at least two carboxyl groups at the terminal group; the monomer M comprises a compound containing two hydroxyls and at least one secondary amine or tertiary amine; and the compound modified with the group E comprises at least one primary amine, secondary amine or tertiary amine group. The in vitro cell-level mRNA delivery efficiency of the cationic polyester of the invention is significantly better than that of the existing commonly used commercial gene transfection reagents, and the cytotoxicity is lower; the cationic polyester is soluble in ethanol, and is easy to be industrialized and clinically applied in preparations, and therefore has great practical application prospects."}},{"_id":"CN-118688333-A","data":{"title":"Marker related to allergic rhinitis and kit thereof","nbCompounds":50,"abstract":"The invention discloses a marker related to allergic rhinitis and a kit thereof. The marker is one or more than one arbitrary combination of 45 different metabolites. The marker has higher specificity and sensitivity, can be used for distinguishing AR patients from normal people, is used for early detection of AR, and strives time for early intervention and treatment for patients."}},{"_id":"DE-102009024542-A1","data":{"title":"Compositions based on chitosan oligosaccharides","nbCompounds":0,"abstract":"The invention relates to compositions containing a chitosan oligosaccharide and at least one drug. Such compositions make it possible to administer the active ingredients to the patient via the skin / mucous membrane. In this way, side effects by bypassing the gastrointestinal tract can be avoided. In addition, a synergism in the effect is observed."}},{"_id":"EP-0046270-A1","data":{"title":"Novel derivatives of bio-affecting phenolic compounds and pharmaceutical composition containing them","nbCompounds":434,"abstract":"Novel transient prodrug forms of bio-affecting phenolic compounds are selected from the group consisting of those having the structural formula (I):n     wherein X is O, S or NR 5  wherein R 5  is hydrogen or lower alkyl; R 2  is selected from the group consisting of straight or branched chain alkyl, aryl, cycloalkyl, alkenyl, cycloalkenyl, alkynyl, aralkyl, alkaryl, aralkenyl, aralkynyl, alkenylaryl, alkynylaryl, (lower acyloxy)alkyl, and carboxyalkyl; These compounds are advantageous resistance modulators."}},{"_id":"EP-2001877-A1","data":{"title":"Method for extracting target alkaloid using an ionic liquid as extracting solvent","nbCompounds":288,"abstract":"Method for extracting a target alkaloid from a mixture of species, typically from plant matter, using an ionic liquid as an extracting solvent. The ionic liquid may in particular be an alkanolyl-, alkoxyalkyl- or aminoalkyl-substituted ammonium salt."}},{"_id":"EP-2054416-A1","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, a process for their preparation and their use as medicine","nbCompounds":700,"abstract":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidine derivatives of formula (I) wherein Y1, Y2, Y3, Y4 represent N or C-, whereby at least two of the groups Y1 to Y4 denote a carbon atom, R1 represents chloro or bromo, R2 to R7 represent e.g. hydrogen, methyl or ethyl; and R10 and R11 independent ly represent e.g. hydrogen or C1-C6alkyl, are potent mGluR5 modulators and are useful for the prevention of acute and chronic neurological disorders."}},{"_id":"EP-2054416-B1","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, a process for their preparation and their use as medicine","nbCompounds":732,"abstract":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidine derivatives of formula (I) wherein Y1, Y2, Y3, Y4 represent N or C-, whereby at least two of the groups Y1 to Y4 denote a carbon atom, R1 represents chloro or bromo, R2 to R7 represent e.g. hydrogen, methyl or ethyl; and R10 and R11 independent ly represent e.g. hydrogen or C1-C6alkyl, are potent mGluR5 modulators and are useful for the prevention of acute and chronic neurological disorders."}},{"_id":"EP-2090576-A1","data":{"title":"6-halo-pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyridines, a process for their preparation and their use as metabotropic glutamate receptor (mGluR) modulators","nbCompounds":1579,"abstract":"The invention relates to 6-halo-pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyridines of formula (I) as well as their pharmaceutically acceptable salts. The invention further relates to a process for the preparation of such compounds. The compounds of the invention are mGluR5 modulators and are therefore useful for the control and prevention of acute and/or chronic neurological disorders.n     nwherein A represents -NR 3 R 4   nwith R 3  and R 4  as described herein."}},{"_id":"EP-2295439-A1","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, a process for their preparation and their use as medicine","nbCompounds":709,"abstract":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidine derivatives of formula (I)n     nwherein nR 1  represents chloro or bromo, nR 2  to R7 represent e.g. hydrogen, methyl or ethyl; and nR 10  and R 11  independently represent e.g. hydrogen or C 1 -C 6 alkyl, nare potent mGluR5 modulators and are useful for the prevention of acute and chronic neurological disorders."}},{"_id":"EP-2440179-A2","data":{"title":"Chitosan oligosaccharide-based compositions","nbCompounds":38,"abstract":"The invention relates to compositions containing a chitosan oligosaccharide and at least one pharmaceutical product. Said compositions allow active ingredients to be administered transdermally to patients. As the gastrointestinal tract is bypassed, side effects can be prevented. In addition, a synergistic action can be observed."}},{"_id":"EP-2440179-B1","data":{"title":"Chitosan oligosaccharide-based compositions","nbCompounds":44}},{"_id":"EP-2849769-A1","data":{"title":"Method for producing a plant extract from desmodium and its extract","nbCompounds":16,"abstract":"This invention relates to a method for producing a plant extract, quantified on pinitol, wherein a plant  Desmodium adscendens  is selected from the  Desmodium  family, wherein a fraction is extracted from  Desmodium  plant parts, wherein a plant extract is derived from said fraction thereof, which is remarkable in that a characterised extract is derived from  Desmodium adscendens , from which a preparation of said plant extract is quantified on pinitol."}},{"_id":"EP-2849769-B1","data":{"title":"Method for producing a plant extract from desmodium and its extract","nbCompounds":15}},{"_id":"JP-2010500288-A","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, methods for their preparation and their use as pharmaceuticals","nbCompounds":510,"abstract":"Formula (I)   [Chemical 1]       (Where        Y 1, Y 2, Y 3      , Y 4 represents N or C-, whereby at least two groups Y  1  to Y  4  represents a carbon atom, R  1  represents a chloro or bromo,   R  2 to  R  7  represent for example hydrogen, methyl or ethyl; and R  10  and R  11  independently represent, for example, hydrogen or C  1  -C  6  alkyl)   Of the substituted pyrazolopyrimidine derivatives are potent mGluR5 modulators and are useful in preventing acute and chronic neurological disorders."}},{"_id":"JP-2015515457-A","data":{"title":"Method for producing plant extract from desmodium and extract thereof","nbCompounds":30,"abstract":"The present invention relates to a pinitol, wherein a desmodium adscendens plant is selected from the Desmodium family, a fraction is obtained by performing extraction from the plant part of the desmodium, and a plant extract is obtained from the fraction. A method of producing a quantified plant extract, which relates to a method wherein a characteristic extract is obtained from Desmodium adsendence, whereby the formulation of said plant extract is quantified relative to pinitol."}},{"_id":"JP-6256932-B2","data":{"title":"Analytical pretreatment method for samples containing multiple components with greatly different concentrations","nbCompounds":72}},{"_id":"JP-WO2017115841-A1","data":{"title":"Analytical pretreatment method for samples containing multiple components with greatly different concentrations","nbCompounds":72,"abstract":"A first holding step of supplying a predetermined amount of the sample and the first solvent to the solid phase and holding the low concentration component on the solid phase, a first holding the first solvent to the solid phase and removing the high concentration component from the solid phase A second holding step in which a small amount of the sample and the second solvent are supplied to the solid phase and the high concentration component is held in the solid phase, and the component held in the solid phase is an extrusion solvent. The first solvent is capable of dissolving the low-concentration component and the high-concentration component, has the characteristics that the low-concentration component is retained in the solid phase, and the high-concentration component is not retained, The two solvents have the property of holding the low-concentration component and the high-concentration component in the solid phase, and it is possible to obtain an analytical sample in which the concentrations of multiple components in the sample that are greatly different in concentration are adjusted to a predetermined range Analysis pretreatment method."}},{"_id":"KR-101955141-B1","data":{"title":"Use of isoquinoline derivatives for the treatment of diabetic wounds","nbCompounds":0,"abstract":"The present invention provides a method of treating a diabetic wound comprising administering to a subject in need of a diabetic wound healing a pharmaceutical composition comprising a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier and a therapeutically effective amount of a compound of formula (I), preferably salsinol, ≪ / RTI >"}},{"_id":"KR-20090047450-A","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, a process for their (ALDH2) activity; and pharmaceutical compositions comprising the compounds. The present invention further provides assays for identifying agonists of ALDH2."}},{"_id":"KR-20110082180-A","data":{"title":"Modulators of aldehyde dehydrogenase and methods of use thereof","nbCompounds":490,"abstract":"The present invention provides compounds and preparations comprising the compounds as well as compounds that act as modulators of aldehyde dehydrogenase (ALDH) enzyme activity. The present disclosure provides a method of treatment involving administering a subject compound, or subject pharmaceutical composition."}},{"_id":"KR-20170110158-A","data":{"title":"Use of isoquinoline derivatives for the treatment of diabetic wounds","nbCompounds":0,"abstract":"The present invention provides a method of treating a diabetic wound comprising administering to a subject in need of a diabetic wound healing a pharmaceutical composition comprising a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier and a therapeutically effective amount of a compound of formula (I), preferably salsinol, ≪ / RTI >"}},{"_id":"KR-20190129765-A","data":{"title":"Method for diagnosing rheumatoid arthritis by using metabolomics and biomarker composition","nbCompounds":0,"abstract":"The present invention relates to a method for gender-specific diagnosis of liver disease or liver cancer by measuring the concentration of salsolinol, which is a metabolite in a biological sample, and comparing the same with the concentration of salsolinol in normal individuals. The present invention not only can more accurately diagnose liver disease or liver cancer derived from alcohol, but also can be usefully used in gender specific biomarker compositions and kits."}},{"_id":"RU-2656225-C1","data":{"title":"Method for producing pyrrolo[2,1-a]isoquinolines","nbCompounds":15,"abstract":"FIELD: chemistry. n     SUBSTANCE: invention relates to the field of organic chemistry, specifically to a process for the preparation of pyrrolo[2,1- a ]isoquinolines 3-6, where 3: R 1 =OCH 3 , R 2 =H, R 3 =Cl, R 4 =H; 4: R 1 =OCH 3 , R 2 =H, R 3 =Cl, R 4 =CH 3 ; 5: R 1 =R 2 =R 3 =OC 2 H 5 , R 4 =H; 6: R 1 =R 2 =R 3 =OC 2 H 5 , R 4 =CH 3 , comprising the dissolution of 6,7-dimethoxy-3,4-dihydro-1-(4-chlorobenzoyl)isoquinoline or 6,7-diethoxy-3,4-dihydro-1-(3,4-diethoxybenzoyl)isoquinoline in acetonitrile and the addition of 1,5-diphenyl-1-penten-4-yn-3-one or 1-(4-methylphenyl)-5-phenyl-1-penten-4-yn-3-one and 30 mol% of copper (I) bromide and exposure to microwave irradiation (300 W) for 30 min at +200 °C, the residue obtained after completion of the reaction and removal of the solvent is crystallised from the ester.   3-6. n     EFFECT: method has been developed for the preparation of pyrrolo[2,1- a ]isoquinolines, which may have useful biological properties. n     1 cl, 4 ex"}},{"_id":"TW-202038972-A","data":{"title":"Heat stress alleviating agent","nbCompounds":0,"abstract":"The present invention addresses the problem of providing a novel heat stress alleviation agent that can be taken simply, safely, and easily, even by infants and the elderly, who are susceptible to heat stress such as heatstroke, that rapidly promotes sweating in heat stress environments, and that prevents, alleviates, or improves symptoms and disorders caused by heat stress, such as heatstroke. Provided are: a heat stress alleviation agent that contains glycosyl hesperidin as an active ingredient; and a basal body temperature maintenance agent that contains glycosyl hesperidin as an active ingredient."}},{"_id":"TW-I715536-B","data":{"title":"Orally-administrable beautifying agent","nbCompounds":40,"abstract":"The subject of the present invention is to provide an oral cosmetic agent for improving the yellow dullness, wrinkles and skin texture of the skin.nn     n   n   n     n      The solution to the problem is to provide an oral cosmetic agent that uses glycoside hesperetin as an active ingredient and is used to improve the yellowness, wrinkles, and skin texture of the skin to solve the aforementioned problems."}},{"_id":"UA-120895-C2","data":{"title":"PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOSITION IN THE FORM OF SYRUP FOR THE TREATMENT OF ALLERGIC DISEASES","nbCompounds":15,"abstract":"The invention relates to pharmacy and medicine, in particular to pharmaceutical compositions consisting of components of synthetic and vegetable origin, made mainly in the form of syrup and exhibiting antihistamine and hepatoprotective action."}},{"_id":"UA-120896-C2","data":{"title":"PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOSITION IN THE FORM OF ORAL SUSPENSION OF ANTIMICROBIAL AND HEPATOPROTECTOR ACTION","nbCompounds":15,"abstract":"The invention relates to pharmacy and medicine, in particular to pharmaceutical compositions consisting of components of synthetic and vegetable origin, made mainly in the form of an oral suspension and exhibiting antimicrobial and hepatoprotective action."}},{"_id":"UA-135478-U","data":{"title":"PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOSITION IN THE FORM OF SYRUP FOR THE TREATMENT OF ALLERGIC DISEASES","nbCompounds":16,"abstract":"A pharmaceutical composition in the form of a syrup for the treatment of allergic diseases, which includes loratadine, pharmaceutically acceptable excipients and purified water. The composition further comprises a dry extract from the terrestrial part of the goldenrod or a dry extract from the terrestrial part of the hill chicken."}},{"_id":"UA-135479-U","data":{"title":"PHARMACEUTICAL COMPOSITION IN THE FORM OF ORAL SUSPENSION OF ANTIMICROBIAL AND HEPATOPROTECTOR ACTION","nbCompounds":15,"abstract":"Pharmaceutical composition in the form of an oral suspension of antimicrobial and hepatoprotective action, which includes sulfamethoxazole, trimethoprim, pharmaceutically acceptable excipients and purified water. The composition further comprises a dry extract from the terrestrial part of the goldenrod or a dry extract from the terrestrial part of the hill chicken."}},{"_id":"US-10161929-B2","data":{"title":"Theranostics platform and methods of use","nbCompounds":2059,"abstract":"Theranostics platforms for identifying drugs and nutraceuticals for treatment of rare disease are described. The platforms comprise (a) a cell-phenotype image-enhancing instrument; (b) a drug/nutraceutical library; and (c) a computer-implemented system for analyzing a response of an optically-visible rare-disease cell phenotype to a drug or nutraceutical from the drug/nutraceutical library."}},{"_id":"US-10656126-B2","data":{"title":"Pre-analysis treatment method for sample containing plurality of components with greatly different concentrations","nbCompounds":180,"abstract":"A pre-analysis treatment method includes: a first retaining step of supplying, to a solid phase, each predetermined amount of the sample and a first solvent, and allowing a component at low concentration to be retained in the solid phase; a first washing step of supplying the first solvent to the solid phase and removing a component at high concentration from the solid phase; a second retaining step of supplying the sample in a smaller amount than the predetermined amount supplied in the first retaining step, and a second solvent to the solid phase, and allowing the component at high concentration to be retained in the solid phase; and an elution step of simultaneously extruding the components retained in the solid phase with an extrusion solvent."}},{"_id":"US-11164733-B2","data":{"title":"Fabrication of mass spectrometry surface","nbCompounds":252,"abstract":"Disclosed herein are compositions for ionizing a target and methods for making the compositions. In some embodiments, the compositions can include a structured substrate having a plurality of upright surface features, for example, microscale or nanoscale pillars, in contact with an initiator. Also disclosed herein are methods for ionizing targets."}},{"_id":"US-2008039458-A1","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, a process for their preparation and their use as medicine","nbCompounds":846}},{"_id":"US-2009143279-A1","data":{"title":"Methods and compositions for treating metabolic disorders","nbCompounds":4181,"abstract":"The present invention provides methods of treating of disorders characterized by defective mitochondrial activity. In particular compounds of the present invention can be used in the treatment metabolic diseases and neurodegenerative diseases. The methods are also useful to increase oxidative phosphorylation or to decrease reactive oxygen species (ROS) production in a subject in need thereof."}},{"_id":"US-2009269772-A1","data":{"title":"Systems and methods for identifying combinations of compounds of therapeutic interest","nbCompounds":3116}},{"_id":"US-2011212956-A1","data":{"title":"Pyrazolopyrimidines for treating CNS disorders","nbCompounds":773}},{"_id":"US-2014050694-A1","data":{"title":"Method of Treating Transplant Rejection and Autoimmune Diseases","nbCompounds":2502}},{"_id":"US-2015050368-A1","data":{"title":"Method for producing a plant extract from desmodium and its extract thereof","nbCompounds":93,"abstract":"This invention relates to a method for producing a plant extract, quantified on pinitol, wherein a plant  Desmodium adscendens  is selected from the  Desmodium  family, wherein a fraction is extracted from  Desmodium  plant parts, wherein a plant extract is derived from said fraction thereof, which is remarkable in that a characterised extract is derived from  Desmodium adscendens , from which a preparation of said plant extract is quantified on pinitol."}},{"_id":"US-2018042899-A1","data":{"title":"Method of Treating Transplant Rejection and Autoimmune Diseases","nbCompounds":3537}},{"_id":"US-2018209945-A1","data":{"title":"Pre-analysis treatment method for sample containing plurality of components with greatly different concentrations","nbCompounds":143,"abstract":"A pre-analysis treatment method includes: a first retaining step of supplying, to a solid phase, each predetermined amount of the sample and a first solvent, and allowing a component at low concentration to be retained in the solid phase; a first washing step of supplying the first solvent to the solid phase and removing a component at high concentration from the solid phase; a second retaining step of supplying the sample in a smaller amount than the predetermined amount supplied in the first retaining step, and a second solvent to the solid phase, and allowing the component at high concentration to be retained in the solid phase; and an elution step of simultaneously extruding the components retained in the solid phase with an extrusion solvent."}},{"_id":"US-2018254177-A1","data":{"title":"Fabrication of mass spectrometry surface","nbCompounds":241,"abstract":"Disclosed herein are compositions for ionizing a target and methods for making the compositions. Also disclosed herein are methods for ionizing targets."}},{"_id":"US-2023277125-A1","data":{"title":"Breath-based therapeutic drug monitoring method","nbCompounds":105,"abstract":"The present invention relates to an ex vivo method and means for determining the risk estimate of side effects of a drug and/or probability estimate of drug response in a subject, the method comprising collecting the mass spectra of substances present in the exhaled breath and analyzing the mass spectra using a previously trained mathematical model. The instant methods and means are particularly useful in therapeutic drug monitoring, determining the risk estimate of side effects of a drug, determining the probability estimate of drug response in the subject, and determination of subject compliance."}},{"_id":"US-5001115-A","data":{"title":"Prodrugs of biologically active hydroxyaromatic compounds","nbCompounds":480,"abstract":"Prodrugs of bio-active hydroxyaromatic drugs having the structural formula: n     A pharmaceutically acceptable prodrug of a biologically active, therapeutically effective hydroxyaromatic drug, said prodrug being selected from the group consisting of, (A) compounds having the structural formula: n     n         DRUG--O--CR'R\"--Z].sub.n n    n     wherein: n     DRUG --O-- is the hydroxyaromatic O-dehydro residue of said drug; n     R' and R' may be the same or different and may be H, alkyl, aryl or electron withdrawing groups; n     Z is a displaceable leaving group; and n     n is an integer in the range of from 1 to 3, and (B) pharmaceutically acceptable salts thereof."}},{"_id":"US-5272270-A","data":{"title":"Process for the preparation of 1-alkylisoquinoline derivatives","nbCompounds":161}},{"_id":"US-5668131-A","data":{"title":"Substituted aminoalkylaminopyridines","nbCompounds":287,"abstract":"Compounds of formula I ##STR1## wherein R1, R2, R3, R4, R5, R6, X, n and A have the meanings as given in the description, can be used for combatting Helicobacter bacteria."}},{"_id":"US-5925642-A","data":{"title":"Pyridopyrimidines","nbCompounds":224,"abstract":"PCT No. PCT/EP96/05046 Sec. 371 Date May 21, 1998 Sec. 102(e) Date May 21, 1998 PCT Filed Nov. 16, 1996 PCT Pub. The ionic liquid may in particular be an alkanolyl-, alkoxyalkyl- or aminoalkyl-substituted ammonium salt."}},{"_id":"WO-2008015269-A1","data":{"title":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidines, a process for their preparation and their use as medicine","nbCompounds":727,"abstract":"Substituted pyrazolopyrimidine derivatives of formula (I) wherein Y1, Y2, Y3, Y4 represent N or C-, whereby at least two of the groups Y1 to Y4 denote a carbon atom, R1 represents chloro or bromo, R2 to R7 represent e.g. hydrogen, methyl or ethyl; and R10 and R11 independent ly represent e.g. hydrogen or C1-C6alkyl, are potent mGluR5 modulators and are useful for the prevention of acute and chronic neurological disorders."}},{"_id":"WO-2009095253-A1","data":{"title":"6-halo-pyrazolo[1, 5-a]pyridines, a process for their preparation and their use as metabotropic glutamate receptor (mglur) modulators","nbCompounds":1770,"abstract":"The invention relates to 6-halo-pyrazolo[1, 5-a] pyridines of formula (I) as well as their pharmaceutically acceptable salts. The invention further relates to a process for the preparation of such compounds. The compounds of the invention are mGluR5 modulators and are therefore useful for the control and prevention of acute and/or chronic neurological disorders."}},{"_id":"WO-2010142400-A2","data":{"title":"Chitosan oligosaccharide-based compositions","nbCompounds":34,"abstract":"The invention relates to compositions containing a chitosan oligosaccharide and at least one pharmaceutical product. Said compositions allow active ingredients to be administered transdermally to patients. As the gastrointestinal tract is bypassed, side effects can be prevented. In addition, a synergistic action can be observed."}},{"_id":"WO-2013089689-A1","data":{"title":"Constituent of desmodium elegans and methods of use","nbCompounds":50}},{"_id":"WO-2013123403-A1","data":{"title":"Theranostics platform and methods of use","nbCompounds":1118,"abstract":"Theranostics platforms for identifying drugs and nutraceuticals for treatment of rare disease are described. The platforms comprise (a) a cell-phenotype image-enhancing instrument; (b) a drug/nutraceutical library; and (c) a computer-implemented system for analyzing a response of an optically- visible rare-disease cell phenotype to a drug or nutraceutical from the drug/nutraceutical library."}},{"_id":"WO-2013166563-A1","data":{"title":"Method for producing a plant extract from desmodium and Desmodium  family, wherein a fraction is extracted from  Desmodium  plant parts, wherein a plant extract is derived from said fraction thereof, which is remarkable in that a characterised extract is derived from  Desmodium adscendens , from which a preparation of said plant extract is quantified on pinitol."}},{"_id":"WO-2013166563-A9","data":{"title":"Method for producing a plant extract from the  Desmodium  family, wherein a fraction is extracted from  Desmodium  plant parts, wherein a plant extract is derived from said fraction thereof, which is remarkable in that a characterised extract is derived from  Desmodium adscendens , from which a preparation of said plant extract is quantified on pinitol."}},{"_id":"WO-2015198346-A1","data":{"title":"A composition comprising extract of alangium salvifolium having anti-adipogenic or anti-obesic activity","nbCompounds":16,"abstract":"The invention relates to herbal anti-adipogenic and pro-lipolytic composition(s) or phytochemical ingredients(s) comprising at least one component selected from the extract(s), fraction(s) and active compound(s) derived from  Alangium salvifolium  either alone or in combination with atleast one component selected from pharmaceutically or dietetically acceptable phytochemical actives, vehicle, diluent and carrier. The present novel ingredients or compositions can be used to control or treat overweight, obesity, metabolic syndrome or other metabolic disorders, regulate energy expenditure, prevention of atherosclerotic plaques in coronary artery and abdominal aorta, increase insulin sensitivity, improve glucose tolerance, lower triglyceride levels and balance glucose levels in mammals."}},{"_id":"WO-2017115841-A1","data":{"title":"Analysis preprocessing method for sample containing multiple constituents in significantly different concentrations","nbCompounds":0,"abstract":"An analysis preprocessing method with which it is possible to obtain an analysis sample in which the concentrations of multiple constituents, which are included in said sample in significantly different concentrations, have been adjusted to predetermined ranges, said method comprising: a first holding step in which predetermined amounts of a sample and a first solvent are supplied to a solid phase, and a low-concentration constituent is held in the solid phase; a first washing step in which the first solvent is supplied to the solid phase, and a high-concentration constituent is removed from the solid phase; a second holding step in which the sample and a second solvent are supplied to the solid phase in smaller amounts than at the first holding step, and the high-concentration constituent is held in the solid phase; and an eluting step in which the constituents held in the solid phase are collectively extracted by an extraction solvent. The first solvent can dissolve the low-concentration constituent and the high-concentration constituent, and has a property for holding the low-concentration constituent in the solid phase and not holding the high-concentration constituent. The second solvent has a property for holding the low-concentration constituent and the high-concentration constituent in the solid phase."}},{"_id":"WO-9515324-A1","data":{"title":"Substituted aminoalkylaminopyridines","nbCompounds":136}},{"_id":"WO-9600224-A1","data":{"title":"4-s- and n-substituted 6-alkylpyridines for controlling helicobacter bacteria","nbCompounds":560,"abstract":"Compounds having the formula (I), in which the substituents and symbols have the meanings given in the description, are useful for controlling helicobacter bacteria."}},{"_id":"WO-9719946-A1","data":{"title":"Pyridopyrimidines","nbCompounds":162,"abstract":"The invention concerns compounds of formula (I) in which the substituents and symbols have the meanings given in the description. These compounds are advantageous resistance modulators."}}]}
